title: David Mead (rugby league)

web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/David-Mead/|title=Players – Love Rugby League|website=Loverugbyleague.com|access-date=4 October 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/david-mead/summary.html|title=David Mead – Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=4 October 2017}} | updated = 9 November 2023 }} '''David Mead''' (born '''David Moore''' on 4 November 1988) is a Papua New Guinean former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|wg}}er, {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at international level. Mead previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos|Broncos]] in the NRL, and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He is a [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country Origin]] and [[World All Stars]] representative, he represented Papua New Guinea at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008]], [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]]s and was the PNG Kumuls' captain for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]].{{Cite web|url=http://www.looppng.com/sport/mead-commends-forwards-68695|title=Mead commends forwards|date=30 October 2017}} ==Background== He was born in [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea as David Moore on 4 November 1988, and moved to Australia as a 12-year-old where he attended [[Kadina High School]] and [[Trinity Catholic College, Lismore|Trinity Catholic College]] in [[Lismore, New South Wales]].{{cite web|last=Mascord|first=Steve|date=3 May 2013|title=MEN OF THE WORLD Two: DAVID MEAD (Papua New Guinea & Australia)|url=http://stevemascord.com/2013/05/03/men-of-the-world-two-david-mead-papua-new-guinea-australia/|website=Stevemascord.com|access-date=4 October 2017|archive-date=5 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171005051152/https://stevemascord.com/2013/05/03/men-of-the-world-two-david-mead-papua-new-guinea-australia/|url-status=dead}} As a junior rugby league footballer, he played for Lismore Marist Brothers before being signed by the [[Gold Coast Titans]],{{cite web |last=Blomberg|first=Matt|date=4 June 2010|title=Mead on Raider alert |work=Northern Star|url=http://www.northernstar.com.au/news/mead-on-raider-alert/549378/}} and playing in their [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2008. He later changed his surname to Mead to honour his aunt's family who had raised him when he moved to Australia.{{cite web|last=Marshall|first=Matt|date=30 January 2009|title=David Mead, Esi Tonga, Kevin Gordon stay at Coast|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/youngsters-stay-with-titans/story-e6frep7o-1111118711629|work=Courier Mail}} [[File:David Moore.jpg|right|thumb|Mead as David Moore in 2008]] ==Playing career== ===2008=== He was named in the Papua New Guinea squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]{{hsp}}{{cite news|date=14 October 2008|title=Wilshere handed Kumuls captaincy|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7669326.stm|publisher=BBC News}} and between the 2008 World Cup and the [[2009 NRL season]] he changed his surname from Moore to Mead. ===2009=== On 30 January 2009, Mead signed a contract extension with the Gold Coast after turning down an offer from the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{cite web|date=30 January 2009|title=Titans Youngsters Stay Loyal|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/titans-youngsters-stay-loyal/story-e6frexr9-1111118713475|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} In round 13 of the [[2009 NRL season]], Mead made his NRL debut for the Gold Coast against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] off the interchange bench in a 28–24 win at [[Robina Stadium|Skilled Park]].{{cite web|last=Clark|first=Laine|date=8 June 2009|title=Titans Defeat Illawarra 28–24|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/gold-coast-titans-defeat-st-george-illawarra-28-24/story-fn2mcuj6-1225729872118|publisher=[[Fox Sports]]}} In his next appearance in round 16 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], Mead made his first appearance in the starting line-up, scoring his first and second NRL career tries in the 28–12 win at Skilled Park.{{cite web|last=Herning|first=Wayne|date=27 June 2009|title=Young Guns Inspire Depleted Gold Coast Titans to Big Win over Warriors|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/young-guns-inspire-depleted-gold-coast-titans-to-big-win-over-warriors/story-fn2mcuj6-1225741652678|publisher=[[Fox Sports]]}} Mead finished his debut year in the NRL with 14 matches and 8 tries. He was part of the Papua New Guinea squad for the [[2009 Pacific Cup]],{{cite web|author=mlipen|date=11 October 2009|title=Kumuls Named|url=http://www.thenational.com.pg/kumuls-named/|work=The National}}{{cite web|date=19 October 2009|title=PNG and Fiji finalise squads for Pacific Cup|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57134/Default.aspx|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=3 September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120927034428/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57134/Default.aspx|archive-date=27 September 2012|url-status=dead}} and was named their player of the year by the [[Rugby League International Federation]].{{cite web|last=Webster|first=Andrew|date=10 November 2009|title=Hayne Named Player of the Year|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} ===2010=== A regular on the wing in 2010, Mead was one of the club's leaders in many statistics before a foot injury saw him miss the rest of the season from round 16 onwards. Mead said, "It is pretty frustrating because I have worked so hard and to get halfway through the year now and it is all just gone, just like that."{{cite web |url=http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2010/06/30/233771_gold-coast-lead-story.html |title=Mead's season in ruins Gold Coast Top Story | goldcoast.com.au | Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia |website=goldcoast.com.au |access-date=27 January 2022 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130419102213/http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2010/06/30/233771_gold-coast-lead-story.html |archive-date=19 April 2013 |url-status=dead}} In Round 12 against the Sydney Roosters, Mead scored a hat trick of tries in the Titans 30–16 loss.{{cite web|last=Nicolussi|first=Christian|date=1 June 2010|title=Sydney Roosters beat Gold Coast Titans 30–16 in NRL round 12 match|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/sydney-roosters-beat-gold-coast-titans-30-16-in-nrl-round-12-match/story-e6frep5x-1225873720312|work=Courier Mail}} Mead played in 15 matches and scored 6 tries during [[2010 NRL season|2010]]. [[File:David Mead.jpg|right|thumb|Mead playing for the Gold Coast Titans in 2011]] ===2011=== Despite the Titans poor record in [[2011 NRL season|2011]] where they finished with the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]], Mead played in every match and was the club's top tryscorer with 16 tries from 24 appearances. In round 22 against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], Mead scored what some considered the best try of the year when he collected a [[Scott Prince]] cross-over kick on the fly behind his back one handed to score in the 20–16 win at [[Endeavour Field|Toyota Stadium]]. Assistant coach Steve Murphy said, "I can't believe he caught that ball. It was just one of those freak things, those amazing things. He's got those in him, Dave."{{cite web|last=Rayner|first=Tristan|date=7 August 2011|title=David Mead scores incredible NRL try|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2011/08/07/david-mead-scores-incredible-nrl-try-video/|publisher=The Roar}}{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} ===2012=== Mead played in 20 matches and scored 10 tries for the Titans in the [[2012 NRL season]]. On 6 December 2012, Mead extended his contract with the Gold Coast for 4 years till the end of the 2016 season.{{cite web|author=titans.com.au|date=6 December 2012|title=Mead re-signs with Titans until 2016|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/mead-resigns-titans-2016-8203/|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=27 June 2015|archive-date=25 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160125195147/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/mead-resigns-titans-2016-8203/|url-status=dead}} ===2013=== In round 23 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], Mead became the first Gold Coast Titans player to score 50 tries for the club during their 22–10 loss at [[Willows Sports Complex|1300SMILES Stadium]].{{cite web|last=Badel|first=Peter|date=18 August 2013|title=North Queensland Cowboys beat Gold Coast Titans 22–10 in NRL round 23|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/north-queensland-cowboys-beat-gold-coast-titans-22-10-in-nrl-round-23/story-fni3fbgz-1226699040076|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} Mead played in 16 matches and scored 11 tries in [[2013 NRL season|2013]]. In October, Mead represented Papua New Guinea in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], playing in all 3 matches at {{rlp|fb}}. ===2014=== In January, Mead was selected for the Titans inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad.{{cite web|last=Meyn|first=Travis|date=22 January 2014|title=Gold Coast Titans to unleash their best team against the Auckland Nines|url=http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-titans-to-unleash-their-best-team-against-the-auckland-nines/story-fnj9yd9w-1226807444043|publisher=Gold Coast Bulletin|access-date=3 September 2016|archive-date=8 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140308020920/http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-titans-to-unleash-their-best-team-against-the-auckland-nines/story-fnj9yd9w-1226807444043|url-status=dead}} Mead was selected for the [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country Origin]] team in the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] match after sorting out his eligibility for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]].{{cite web|last1=Badel|first1=Peter|last2=Meyn|first2=Travis|date=30 April 2014|title=David Mead a Kumul chameleon after the Papua New Guinea international changes eligibility|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/david-mead-a-kumul-chameleon-after-the-papua-new-guinea-international-changes-eligibility/story-fniabjcr-1226900064511|work=Courier Mail}} Mead played at {{rlp|fb}} and scored 2 tries in the 26–26 all draw in [[Dubbo, New South Wales|Dubbo]].{{cite web|last=Toohey|first=Barry|date=4 May 2014|title=Hulking Centre Jamal Idris Stars in Thrilling City/Country Draw|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hulking-centre-jamal-idris-stars-in-thrilling-citycountry-draw-in-dubbo/story-fni3fbgz-1226904860045|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} In round 11 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], Mead played his 100th [[NRL]] career match in the 24–16 loss at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].{{cite web|last=Balym|first=Todd|date=24 May 2014|title=Dave Taylor shines as Gold Coast Titans keep sinking following loss to Warriors|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/dave-taylor-shines-as-gold-coast-titans-keep-sinking-following-loss-to-warriors/story-fniabrr8-1226930400568|work=Courier Mail}} Mead finished the [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing 18 matches and scoring 4 tries, his worst try-scoring effort to date. ===2015=== Mead was again a member of the Titans' [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] squad.{{cite web|last=Luca|first=Rocco|date=20 January 2015|title=Titans name 2015 nines squad|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-2015-nines-squad/|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=27 June 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160124040230/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=24 January 2016|url-status=dead}} He also returned for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]], playing at fullback and scoring a try in their 34–22 win at [[Wagga Wagga]].{{cite web|date=3 May 2015|title=Representative Round team lists|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|publisher=National Rugby League}}{{cite web|last=Brady|first=Jack|date=3 May 2015|title=Country overcome City in seesawing affair|url=http://www.nrl.com/country-overcome-city-in-seesawing-affair/tabid/10874/newsid/85906/default.aspx|publisher=National Rugby League}} In round 25 against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], Mead eclipsed [[Kevin Gordon (rugby league)|Kevin Gordon]]'s record as the Gold Coast Titans all-time highest try-scorer with 60 tries, overtaking Gordon's 58 tries for the club by scoring 2 tries in the Gold Coast's 28–26 win at [[Robina Stadium|Cbus Super Stadium]].{{cite web|last=Meyn|first=Travis|date=24 July 2015|title=Gold Coast Titans duo Kevin Gordon and David Mead want to avoid another wooden spoon|url=http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-titans-duo-kevin-gordon-and-david-mead-want-to-avoid-another-wooden-spoon/story-fnj9yd9w-1227454688358|publisher=Gold Coast Bulletin|access-date=19 December 2015|archive-date=1 August 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150801165907/http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-titans-duo-kevin-gordon-and-david-mead-want-to-avoid-another-wooden-spoon/story-fnj9yd9w-1227454688358|url-status=dead}}{{cite web|last=Lutton|first=Phil|date=30 August 2015|title=Dave Taylor enigma continues as Gold Coast Titans upset St George Illawarra Dragons|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/dave-taylor-enigma-continues-as-gold-coast-titans-upset-st-george-illawarra-dragons-20150830-gjb0lb.html|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}} He scored 5 tries from 22 matches for the [[2015 NRL season]]. ===2016=== On 29 January 2016, Mead was named in the Titans 2016 Auckland Nines squad.{{cite web|last=Luca|first=Rocco|date=29 January 2016|title=Titans unveil 2016 nines squad|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=2 February 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131060105/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=31 January 2016|url-status=dead}} On 7 May 2015, Mead captained [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[International rugby league in 2016#Papua New Guinea vs Fiji men in Australia|2016 Melanesian Cup Test]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]], where he played at fullback in the 24–22 win at [[Parramatta Stadium]].{{cite web|last=Campton|first=Nick|date=7 May 2016|title=Papua New Guinea hold on to upset Fiji in Pacific Test thriller|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/papua-new-guinea-hold-on-to-upset-fiji-in-pacific-test-thriller/news-story/2f752f9bd3573ba524f1c5f1deb0f6e2|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}} On 10 September 2016, it was announced that Mead would sign with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] from 2017 on a one-year deal.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/broncos/nrl-2016-brisbane-broncos-sign-gold-coast-titan-david-mead-for-2017/news-story/ddc941d2a0dc4933ae030340fd78f437|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|publisher=[[Fox Sports]]|date=10 September 2016|access-date=2016-12-05}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/david-mead-why-i-chose-the-broncos/tabid/10874/newsid/101423/default.aspx|title=David Mead: Why I chose the Broncos|publisher=National Rugby League|date=16 September 2016|access-date=2016-12-05}} Mead finished his last year with the Gold Coast Titans with him playing in 18 matches and scoring 7 tries in the [[2016 NRL season]]. On 14 December 2016, Mead was named in the [[World All Stars]] team on the wing to play against the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] on 10 February 2017.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/world-all-stars-indigenous-all-stars-squads-announced-ahead-of-preseason-showpiece/news-story/14b89eeb178b7bb8a8c7451dbfe6fce2|title=NRL All Stars squads: World All Stars line up, Indigenous All Stars line up|date=14 December 2016|publisher=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=4 October 2017}} ===2017=== On 10 February 2017, Mead played for the [[World All Stars]] against the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in the [[2017 All Stars match]], playing at fullback and scoring a try in the 34–8 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-updates-of-nrl-all-stars-at-hunter-stadium-in-newcastle/news-story/d137d71a228da375f0d48a0d0ed1bdaf|title=Rough start for Nine's new footy host|publisher=News.com.au|access-date=4 October 2017}} On 18 February 2017, Mead made his Broncos debut in the [[2017 World Club Series]] match against the [[Warrington Wolves]] where he started on the wing and scored a try in the 27–18 loss at [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/brisbane-broncos-loss-to-warrington-wolves-creates-world-club-series-history/news-story/222583a3ab4c12a192fdae9abf7ed39f|title=Broncos' loss to Wolves creates history|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=4 October 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/warrington-vs-brisbane/45988|title=Warrington 27–18 Brisbane: Wolves' first-half blitz stuns Broncos|publisher=[[Sky Sports]]|date=19 February 2017|access-date=6 April 2017}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38971476|title=World Club Series: Warrington Wolves 27–18 Brisbane Broncos|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=18 February 2017|access-date=30 April 2017}} At the start of the 2017 season, Mead was languishing in the [[Queensland Cup]] playing for the [[Wynnum-Manly Seagulls]] after coach [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] opted to shift [[Jordan Kahu]] to the wing and put Broncos new recruit [[Tautau Moga]] at centre leaving Mead out of the starting backline.{{Cite web |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/the-inside-mail-home-of-all-the-hottest-nrl-news-and-gossip/news-story/38e2bd58503a814eb8486826499bbd02 |title=Archived copy |access-date=2 June 2017 |archive-date=11 March 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220311174023/https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/the-inside-mail-home-of-all-the-hottest-nrl-news-and-gossip/news-story/38e2bd58503a814eb8486826499bbd02 |url-status=dead }} In Round 6 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Mead made his first appearance in the NRL for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] against the [[Sydney Roosters]], filling in for an injured [[Corey Oates]] on the wing and scoring a try in the 32–8 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/broncos-defeat-roosters-328-five-things-we-learned/news-story/826e2a45cc6887f4c5a3e208ac5d2b6a|title=Broncos Defeat Roosters 32–8; Five Things We Learned|website=The Courier-Mail|access-date=4 October 2017}} On 21 April 2017, Mead extended his contract with Brisbane to the end of the 2018 season and also on the same day in round 8 against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Mead played his 150th [[NRL]] career match in Brisbane's 25–24 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://m.nrl.com/mead-re-signs-with-broncos/tabid/10874/newsid/105903/default.aspx|title=Mead re-signs with Broncos|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=4 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170823024500/https://m.nrl.com/mead-re-signs-with-broncos/tabid/10874/newsid/105903/default.aspx|archive-date=23 August 2017|url-status=dead}} Mead finished his year stint at the [[Brisbane Broncos]] with him playing in 12 matches and scoring 3 tries in the [[2017 NRL season]]. On 4 October 2017, Mead was named as the Captain of the Papua New Guinea 24-man squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].{{Cite web | url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/10/04/png-kumuls-name-world-cup-squad/ | title=PNG Kumuls name World Cup squad | date=4 October 2017 }} Mead played in all 4 matches at fullback and scoring 4 tries for the Kumuls, having a great tournament by starting with scoring a hattrick of tries in the 50–6 demolishing victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in [[Port Moresby]].{{cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/png-thrash-wales-506-in-rugby-league-world-cup-clash/news-story/b7cd7b486e3914982ad9e81a959d096d |accessdate=12 February 2022 |title=PNG thrash Wales 50–6 in Rugby League World Cup clash |website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney | date=28 October 2017 }}{{Cite web | url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/10/24/meads-emotional-png-homecoming/ | title=Mead's emotional PNG homecoming | date=23 October 2017 }} On 12 December 2017, Mead announced that he had signed a 3-year deal with the Catalans Dragons in the [[Super League]], starting in 2018.{{Cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/42323094 | title=Catalans sign PNG's Mead from Brisbane | publisher=BBC Sport }} ===2018=== [[File:David Mead Catalans.jpg|right|thumb|250px|Mead warming up for the Catalans Dragons at Wembley in 2018]] He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}} ===2019=== Mead played a total of 17 games for Les Catalans in the [[Super League XXIV]] season as the club finished 7th on the table and missed out on the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1131/2019-clubs-season-graded/|title=2019 clubs season graded|website=Super League}} ===2020=== Mead played 7 games for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the 2020 Super League season including the club's 48–2 semi-final defeat against St Helens.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2020/nov/20/st-helens-catalans-dragons-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens demolish Catalans Dragons to set up Grand Final clash with Wigan|website=The Guardian|date=20 November 2020}} ===2021=== On 13 January, Mead signed for the [[Brisbane Broncos]].{{cite web|publisher=Love Rugby League|date=13 January 2021|access-date=13 January 2021|title=David Mead joins Brisbane|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/david-mead-joins-brisbane/}} In round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored a try in his return game to the NRL but Brisbane lost the match 24–16 against Parramatta.{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-12/brisbane-broncos-lose-to-parramatta-eels-xavier-coates-injured/13242554/|title=Brisbane Broncos' NRL losing streak continues as Parramatta Eels score comeback 24–16 win; Newcastle Knights beat Bulldogs|publisher=ABC News|location=Australia|date=12 March 2021}} In round 11 against the [[Sydney Roosters]], Mead scored a [[hat-trick]] in Brisbane's 34–16 victory.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-sydney-roosters-vs-brisbane-broncos-round-11-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-sam-walker-payne-haas/news-story/7ca81517ad9318ee14b4aedd903e99ae/|title=Broncos
